body:
Thanks for reporting @bflad!

*Unfortunately, the issue doesn't trivially reproduce.* - I tried and it works as expected. Thus, we have to gather more information.

Can you provide the content of `.git/config` (the `[core]` section specifically)?

Can you also provide the possibly slimmed down version of the `zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore` file that still reproduces the issue for you after reducing it?

I have a feeling there is some unprintable character that it picks up that git will ignore.

Thanks again.

> Can you provide the content of .git/config (the [core] section specifically)?

Here's both user and repo config:

```console
$ cat ~/.gitconfig
[user]
	name = Brian Flad
	email = OMITTED
[url "ssh://git@github.com/"]
    insteadOf = https://github.com/

[github]
	user = bflad

$ cat .git/config
[core]
	repositoryformatversion = 0
	filemode = true
	bare = false
	logallrefupdates = true
	ignorecase = true
	precomposeunicode = true
```

> Can you also provide the possibly slimmed down version of the `zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore` file that still reproduces the issue for you after reducing it?

Just a heads up that there are multiple directories containing `.gitignore` before the affected directory if that matters at all.

```console
$ cat .gitignore
.run
scratch/
.vscode/launch.json
.vscode/settings.json
.vscode/tasks.json
.config/
_debug.go
bin/
/debug/
errors.csv
.idea
testprojects/
testusages/
testreadme/*/.README.md
testSDKs/
jsonschemas/
*.iml
vendor
.terraform
.DS_Store
go.work*
/ci/backend.conf
.pytest_cache
.DS_Store
obj/
.ruby-version
openapi-generation.sln
.project
!.prettierrc
node_modules
.settings
.classpath
report.html
__pycache__/
.mono/
__debug_bin*
*.orig
.gh-cache-profile
.spr.yml
__debug__/
**/OMITTED/logs/
OMITTED/generated-files-*
.flox/

$ cat zSDKs/.gitignore
*

!mcp-*/
!mcp-*/**/*
!sdk-*/
!sdk-*/**/*
!terraform-provider-testing/
!terraform-provider-testing/**/*
sdk-typescript*/__tests__
#!httpbin-docs-*/
#!httpbin-docs-*/**/*
.DS_Store
.idea
test-*-record.tx
.classpath
.settings
_debug/

$ cat zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
**/OMITTED/temp/
**/OMITTED/logs/
OMITTED/logs/
OMITTED/temp/
bin/
sorbet/
*.gem
.rbenv-gemsets
.DS_Store
```

If I reduce `zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore` to just `sorbet/`, the issue still reproduces between `gix` and `git`.

```console
$ git status --untracked-files=all
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/OMITTED/logs/naming.log
	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/openapi-0.0.1.gem

no changes added to commit (use "git add" and/or "git commit -a")

$ gix status
 10:55:26 status done 8.9K files in 0.05s (176.0K files/s)
  M zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.speakeasy/logs/
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/openapi-0.0.1.gem
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/annotations/
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/.gitattributes
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/base64@0.3.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/erubi@1.13.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/faraday-multipart@1.1.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/faraday-net_http@3.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/json@2.8.2.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/language_server-protocol@3.17.0.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/lint_roller@1.1.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/logger@1.6.6.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/minitest-focus@1.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/multipart-post@2.4.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/net-http@0.6.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/parallel@1.26.3.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/parser@3.3.7.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/prism@1.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/rack@3.1.11.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/regexp_parser@2.10.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/rubocop-minitest@0.37.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/tapioca@0.16.11.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/thor@1.3.2.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/unicode-display_width@3.1.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/unicode-emoji@4.0.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/uri@1.0.3.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/yard-sorbet@0.9.0.rbi
```

If I update the pattern to `sorbet/**` (regardless of other patterns in that .gitignore file) those files are ignored with both `gix` and `git`.

Thanks so much!

> If I reduce `zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore` to just `sorbet/`, the issue still reproduces between `gix` and `git`.

Could I have that exact, reduced file attached here assuming it still reproduces the issue? I think there might be something wrong with it.

Because I simply can't reproduce it, even when adding all the intermediate .gitignore files presented here.

Thanks again.

Here's more information about that file (attached with renaming of "gitignore.txt"). In case this matters, this is macOS.

[gitignore.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21722434/gitignore.txt)

```console
$ file zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore: ASCII text

$ cat -e zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
sorbet/$
$

$ od -t c zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
0000000    s   o   r   b   e   t   /  \n  \n
0000011

$ hexdump zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
0000000 6f73 6272 7465 0a2f 000a
0000009

$ gix status
 15:48:27 status done 8.9K files in 0.05s (186.2K files/s)
  M zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/OMITTED/logs/
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/openapi-0.0.1.gem
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/annotations/
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/.gitattributes
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/base64@0.3.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/erubi@1.13.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/faraday-multipart@1.1.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/faraday-net_http@3.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/json@2.8.2.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/language_server-protocol@3.17.0.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/lint_roller@1.1.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/logger@1.6.6.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/minitest-focus@1.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/multipart-post@2.4.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/net-http@0.6.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/parallel@1.26.3.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/parser@3.3.7.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/prism@1.4.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/rack@3.1.11.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/regexp_parser@2.10.0.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/rubocop-minitest@0.37.1.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/tapioca@0.16.11.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/thor@1.3.2.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/unicode-display_width@3.1.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/unicode-emoji@4.0.4.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/uri@1.0.3.rbi
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/yard-sorbet@0.9.0.rbi

$ git status
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/OMITTED/logs/
	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/openapi-0.0.1.gem

no changes added to commit (use "git add" and/or "git commit -a")
```

Thanks so much for bearing with me here! The file clearly isn't at fault, and just in case, copying the attached minimized file directly onto mine doesn't change anything either. It can't, as the `od` tool clearly shows.

The thing is… nothing else matters - it traverses the directory and applies ignore rules to untracked files.

Oh wait, I think I got something!

```
❯ gix exclude query -i zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:6:!sdk-*/**/*        zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

This would mean that the file isn't actually excluded even though it definitely doesn't list. What it should say is that `sortbet/` matches. Commenting that line out leads to this, which doesn't make much sense either. This makes me think the `-i` flag doesn't work as I think it does:

```
❯ gix exclude query -i zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:1:*  zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo

❯ gix exclude query zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:1:*  zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

But then again, also removing this one leads to the expected result:

```
❯ gix exclude query  zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore:1:sorbet/   zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

Funnily enough, no matter what I do, the files are always effectively ignored for me, probably because `sorbet/` is ignored.

```
❯ gix exclude query zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet
./zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore:1:sorbet/   zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet
```

What happens when you run these commands? Is `zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/` ignored? Is there a subtle casing issue?

Also, what happens if `core.ignorecase` is set to `false`?

Here's what I get in my environment (baseline `.gitignore` files / `core.ignorecase = true` as it currently is)

```console
$ gix status
 10:10:59 status done 8.9K files in 0.04s (212.5K files/s)
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/annotations/
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/.gitattributes
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/base64@0.3.0.rbi
...
  ? zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/yard-sorbet@0.9.0.rbi

$ gix exclude query -i zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:6:!sdk-*/**/*	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo

$ gix exclude query zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
::	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

After commenting `!sdk-*/**/*` in `zSDKs/.gitignore`

```console
$ gix status
 10:11:55 status done 8.9K files in 0.04s (207.3K files/s)
  M zSDKs/.gitignore

$ gix exclude query -i zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:1:*	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo

$ gix exclude query zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/.gitignore:1:*	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

After commenting `*` in `zSDKs/.gitignore` (and leaving `!sdk-*/**/*` commented):

```console
$ gix status
 10:12:28 status done 8.9K files in 0.05s (196.7K files/s)
  M zSDKs/.gitignore
  ? zSDKs/openapi.yaml
  ? zSDKs/OMITTED
  ? zSDKs/OMITTED
  ? zSDKs/OMITTED

$ gix exclude query -i zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore:6:sorbet/	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo

$ gix exclude query zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
./zSDKs/sdk-ruby/.gitignore:6:sorbet/	zSDKs/sdk-ruby/sorbet/rbi/gems/foo
```

Updating `.git/config` with `core.ignorecase = false` has no effect on the output of these commands. To me it appears Gitoxide is not respecting the deepest/closest .gitignore for ignores/overrides to match Git's behavior, but I'm not familiar with that tool nor its implementation details. Please let me know if there's other troubleshooting/debugging I can do to help.

Thank you!

What's strange here is that `!sdk-*/**/*` indeed seems to be the culprit, and it's showing strange behaviour due to it even for me, nonetheless the exclusion works correctly in my test-setup (which mirrors yours) as exactly as I could make it.

Did we establish that you are using the latest version of `starship`? Also, the `gix` version in use for me is `v0.45.0-2-gb0453803e7`.

If so, I'd think there is already something to look into, at least so that I understand why `sorbet/` isn't always the pattern that matches as I would expect.

This machine uses mainly [Homebrew](https://brew.sh) for installs and it's using the latest `gitoxide` and `starship` formulas from there:

```console
$ starship --version
starship 1.23.0
branch:
commit_hash:
build_time:2025-04-27 15:52:14 +00:00
build_env:rustc 1.86.0 (05f9846f8 2025-03-31) (Homebrew),

$ gix --version
gix 0.45.0

$ git --version
git version 2.39.5 (Apple Git-154)
```

Thank you - it looks like there is no (meaningful) difference in the versions we are using.

Then I am left to understanding how `!sdk-*/**/*` affects everything to the point that it does, and what's going on here, and maybe reproduce the issue in the process of doing so.

Unfortunately it will be a while before that can happen as this seems to be quite an isolated issue till now. There is also #6881 which I'd have to tackle first.
